mnemonics: Object Return Trip: A method sends an object back home, just like a mail carrier returns a package to its sender.
keyPoints: A method can return an object of any class, including user-defined classes.
The return type in the method signature must match the class of the object being returned.
Returning an object allows methods to be factories that create and configure new instances.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to use the 'new' keyword to instantiate the object before returning it, leading to a NullPointerException.
Returning a reference to a mutable object's internal state, breaking encapsulation and allowing external code to modify it directly.
